title: Pillowy Norwegian Sour Cream Flatbread
description: A yeasty, pillowy, lazy-sunday Norwegian dish featuring full-fat sour cream, perfect for a flexitarian breakfast or brunch.
instructions: In a small bowl, combine warm water and yeast, stirring to dissolve. Let it sit for 5-7 minutes until foamy.
In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, yeast mixture, salt, sugar, and melted butter. Mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 10-12 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch.
Transfer the dough to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, and use your fingers to create edges and dimples.
Brush the top with sour cream and sprinkle with chives and caraway seeds.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown and puffed.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
Cut the flatbread into strips, and serve immediately
